commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6a70104ebba9e178a91a5676e05ad2297c4d02b0)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a billing-classified error branch in `applyJobResult` that disables recurring `agentTurn` cron jobs, clears `nextRunAtMs`, logs a warning, and adds focused tests.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +69. Total +91 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main and v2026.6.9 keep recurring errored jobs on the scheduled backoff path, while the PR head early-returns to disable recurring `agentTurn` jobs when `lastErrorReason === "billing"`; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blocker is maintainer policy, upgrade/recovery semantics, and contributor real behavior proof; automation should not choose a fail-closed cron default.

target.title: fix(cron): disable recurring agent jobs after billing errors
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a scheduler-level safety stop for recurring `agentTurn` cron jobs when a run fails with a provider error classified as `billing`.

When a recurring agent cron run records `lastErrorReason === "billing"`, the scheduler now:

- disables the job;
- clears `nextRunAtMs`;
- logs a warning explaining that the recurring agent job was disabled after a billing error.

This prevents unattended recurring agent jobs from continuing to schedule after a billing-classified provider failure.

## What Problem This Solves

Recurring scheduled agent jobs can repeatedly retry after provider-side billing failures. For a recurring agent job, a billing-classified failure is not a transient execution problem: retrying the same schedule without operator intervention can keep the job eligible for future runs even though the account or provider requires billing action.

This change makes recurring `agentTurn` cron jobs fail closed after a billing-classified error by disabling the job and clearing `nextRunAtMs`. One-shot jobs, command jobs, system-event jobs, and rate-limit-only failures keep their existing behavior.

## Evidence

Validation performed on this branch:

- Focused regression tests cover the billing stop and the rate-limit-only non-regression path.
- The mixed billing/rate-limit provider text case disables the recurring agent job, clears `nextRunAtMs`, records `lastErrorReason: "billing"`, and increments `consecutiveErrors`.
- The pure rate-limit case remains enabled and keeps the existing retry/backoff behavior.
- Dedicated Vitest run passed: `pnpm exec vitest run src/cron/service/timer.billing-safety.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.
- Formatting and lint checks passed for the changed files: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...` and `pnpm exec oxlint ...`.
- Changed-file validation passed locally with `pnpm check:changed` in local child mode.
- Manual behavior smoke produced the expected state transition:

```json
{"id":"billing-mixed","enabled":false,"nextRunAtMs":null,"lastErrorReason":"billing","consecutiveErrors":1}
{"id":"pure-rate-limit","enabled":true,"nextRunAtMs":1782112831000,"lastErrorReason":"rate_limit","consecutiveErrors":1}
```

The proof is intentionally source-level and sanitized: it demonstrates the cron state transition without including private deployment details, provider account details, costs, or local infrastructure identifiers.

## Why

Billing failures are different from transient model failures. Retrying or continuing a recurring scheduled agent job after a billing-classified provider error is unlikely to recover automatically and can create unnecessary repeated provider attempts.

This PR intentionally keeps the initial behavior narrow and conservative:

- `billing` is treated as terminal for recurring `agentTurn` jobs;
- `rate_limit`-only errors keep the existing retry/backoff behavior;
- one-shot jobs are not changed;
- command cron jobs are not changed;
- system-event cron jobs are not changed.

## Behavior

Before this PR, a recurring agent cron job with a billing-classified error could remain enabled and continue through the normal recurring error scheduling path.

After this PR:

```text
recurring agentTurn cron + billing-classified error
=> enabled = false
=> nextRunAtMs = undefined
```

A dedicated regression test also covers the mixed-error case where the error text contains both a billing failure and a fallback/rate-limit failure. The billing classification wins and disables the recurring agent job.

## Tests

Dedicated test:

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run src/cron/service/timer.billing-safety.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       2 passed (2)
```

Changed-file validation:

```bash
OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 \
OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 \
CI=1 \
PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false \
pnpm check:changed
```

Result:

```text
EXIT=0
```

The changed-file check covered:

- conflict marker guard;
- changelog attribution guard;
- duplicate scan target coverage;
- dependency pin guard;
- package patch guard;
- core typecheck;
- core test typecheck;
- type-aware lint for changed core files;
- database-first legacy-store guard;
- media download helper guard;
- runtime sidecar loader guard;
- runtime import cycle guard;
- webhook body guard;
- pairing store/account guards.
